info.name: ⚡ ThunderID API
info.description: Complete API collection for ThunderID identity and access management.

description.content: Operations related to agent management

query.description: Maximum number of records to return. Must be between 1 and 100 inclusive.
Number of records to skip for pagination. Must be a non-negative integer.

Filter agents by a custom attribute value. Only keys inside the `attributes` JSON
object are filterable; system fields (`name`, `description`, `type`, `ouId`, `owner`,
`clientId`) are not supported.
Supported operators: `eq`.
Format: `attribute operator "value"`. The value must be wrapped in double quotes.
Example: `region eq "us-east-1"`.
Pass `display` to populate `ouHandle` fields in the response.

description.content: Added as a part of security scheme: oauth2
Added as a part of security scheme: oauth2

response.name: Unauthorized - missing or invalid authentication token

description.content: Added as a part of security scheme: oauth2
Added as a part of security scheme: oauth2

Creates a new agent. At minimum, an entity is created with the provided identity fields.
An inbound auth profile is created when any inbound auth config fields are supplied.
An OAuth profile is created when `inboundAuthConfig` is supplied.

The `clientSecret` is returned once in the response and never again — store it securely.

response.name: Agent created

Full replacement update. `name` and `type` are required. All inbound auth profile
and OAuth profile fields are applied according to the same rules as
CreateAgentRequest. Omitting an inbound auth or OAuth field clears it.

If `ouId` is provided and differs from the current value, the agent is moved to the
new organization unit (which must exist).

Operations related to agent type schema management

Retrieves the list of agent type schemas. Agent types are restricted to a single
bootstrap-provisioned `default` schema; this endpoint returns that one schema.

query.description: Maximum number of records to return. Must be between 1 and 100 inclusive.
Number of records to skip for pagination. Must be a non-negative integer.
Pass `display` to populate `ouHandle` fields in the response.

item.name: Create the default agent type schema

request.name: Create the default agent type schema

description.content:
Creates the agent type schema. The `name` must be `default` — agent types are
restricted to a single `default` schema per deployment, so any other name is
rejected with `USRS-1014`.

description.content:
Updates the agent type schema with new attribute definitions. The `name` must remain
`default` — renaming to anything else is rejected with `USRS-1014`. This operation
replaces the entire schema definition.

item.name: Delete an agent type schema by ID (not supported)

request.name: Delete an agent type schema by ID (not supported)

description.content:
Deleting agent type schemas is not supported. The `default` agent type cannot be
removed; agent creation depends on it. Update the schema in place instead.
This endpoint always returns `USRS-1015`.

description.content: Operations related to resource export
Exports the requested resources and returns a JSON response containing the combined YAML export content and the generated `.env` file content.

response.name: Combined YAML and environment variable export

description.content: Added as a part of security scheme: oauth2
Added as a part of security scheme: oauth2

response.name: Unauthorized - missing or invalid authentication token

description.content: Added as a part of security scheme: oauth2
Added as a part of security scheme: oauth2

Exports the requested resources and returns them as a downloadable ZIP archive. Each exported resource is placed in a separate file (optionally inside a sub-folder determined by `folderPath`). When template variables are detected across the exported resources, a `.env` file is automatically generated and included in the ZIP root; the `.env` file is omitted when no template variables are found.

response.name: ZIP archive containing all exported resource files

description.content: Added as a part of security scheme: oauth2
Added as a part of security scheme: oauth2
Added as a part of security scheme: oauth2
Operations for retrieving flow metadata

Retrieves aggregated metadata for a flow, including:
- Application or OU details based on type
- Resolved design configuration (theme and layout)
- i18n translations based on language and namespace filters
- Registration flow enablement status

**Note:** When `type=APP`, the OU metadata is only included in the response
when the deployment has exactly one organization unit (i.e., `totalResults == 1`).
For deployments with multiple OUs, the OU metadata will be omitted from APP-type responses.

query.description: (Required) The type of entity to retrieve metadata for
(Required) The UUID of the entity (application ID or organization unit ID)
Language tag in BCP 47 format for i18n translations (e.g., `en`, `es`, `fr-CA`)
Filter translations by namespace (e.g., `auth`, `errors`, `common`)

Operations related to group management using hierarchical handle paths

item.name: List groups in organization unit specified by handle path

request.name: List groups in organization unit specified by handle path

query.description: Maximum number of records to return.
Number of records to skip for pagination.

Optional parameter to include additional display information.
- `display` - Include `ouHandle`, the human-readable handle of the organization unit, alongside the `ouId`.

variable.description:
(Required) Hierarchical path of organization unit handles separated by forward slashes.
Examples:
- `engineering` - Lists groups in the "engineering" OU
- `engineering/frontend` - Lists groups in "engineering/frontend"

response.name: List of groups in the organization unit

item.name: Get an organization unit by hierarchical handle path

request.name: Get an organization unit by hierarchical handle path

variable.description:
(Required) Hierarchical path of organization unit handles separated by forward slashes.
Examples:
- `engineering` - Gets the root OU with handle "engineering"
- `engineering/frontend` - Gets the "frontend" OU under "engineering"
- `engineering/frontend/ui` - Gets the "ui" OU under "engineering/frontend"

- `engineering` - Creates a new OU under the "engineering" OU
- `engineering/frontend` - Creates a new OU under "engineering/frontend"

- No parentId: returns only top-level resources (where parent is null)
- parentId={uuid}: returns direct children of specified parent

- UUID: returns direct children of that parent

Returns user and group assignments for the role. Use type to filter by assignee type and include=display to get display names.

query.description: Filter assignments by assignee type. When omitted, assignments of all types are returned.

Optional parameter to include additional information.
- `display` - Include display names for users and groups. For users, the display value is resolved from the schema-configured display attribute (`systemAttributes.display`). Falls back to the user ID if no display attribute is configured, the configured attribute path does not exist in the user's data, or the attribute value is empty. For groups, the group name is used.

query.description: Maximum number of records to return. Must be between 1 and 100 inclusive.
Number of records to skip for pagination. Must be a non-negative integer.

Filter users by attribute values.
Supported operators: eq (ie. equals).
Format: `attribute operator "value"`.
Examples:
- `username eq "john.doe"` - Users with username = "john.doe"'
- `age eq 25` - Users with age = 25
- `address.city eq "Mountain View"` - Users with address.city = "Mountain View"

Optional parameter to include additional display information in the response. The exact fields included depend on the endpoint. See each endpoint's response schema for details on which fields are enriched.

query.description: Maximum number of records to return. Must be between 1 and 100 inclusive.

Filter users by attribute values.
Supported operators: eq (ie. equals).
Format: `attribute operator "value"`.
Examples:
- `username eq "john.doe"` - Users with username = "john.doe"'
- `age eq 25` - Users with age = 25
- `address.city eq "Mountain View"` - Users with address.city = "Mountain View"
